title: 2025-26 EFL League Two - Managerial Changes
text: The 2025-26 EFL League Two season featured significant managerial activity across multiple clubs. Several managers departed their positions while new appointments were made throughout the campaign. Newport County underwent managerial transitions, with David Hughes initially appointed in May 2025 but later replaced by Christian Fuchs in November 2025. Bristol Rovers saw Darrell Clarke take the helm in May 2025, succeeding the previous manager. Notts County appointed Martin Paterson as manager in June 2025. Mid-season changes proved substantial. Cheltenham Town parted ways with Michael Flynn in September 2025, with Steve Cotterill arriving as replacement in late September. Barrow experienced multiple managerial changes, with Paul Gallagher appointed in January 2026, later replaced by Dino Maamria in February, who was then succeeded by Sam Foley in an interim capacity. Bristol Rovers replaced Clarke with Steve Evans in December 2025. Fleetwood Town saw Pete Wild depart, with Matt Lawlor initially taking an interim position before his permanent appointment in January 2026. Shrewsbury Town's Michael Appleton departed by mutual consent in January 2026, with Gavin Cowan becoming the new manager. Tranmere Rovers replaced Andy Crosby with Pete Wild in an interim capacity during March 2026. Walsall appointed Darren Byfield on an interim basis in March 2026, following Mat Sadler's departure. Crawley Town appointed Colin Kazim-Richards as manager in March 2026. The season showcased typical volatility within professional football management, with performance pressures leading to frequent personnel changes across the division.
